title: Crispy Tangy Indian Lime Leaves Crumble
description: A zesty, crumbly topping that pairs with yogurt or salads, packed with lime leaves, a tadka of mustard seeds, and diabetic-friendly sweetener.

ingredients.name: Roasted chickpeas
ingredients.notes: Can use canned chickpeas, drain and dry before roasting

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Spread the chickpeas on a baking sheet and roast for 10‑12 minutes, stirring halfway, until golden and crisp. Let cool.
In a large bowl, combine the roasted chickpeas, shredded coconut, erythritol, cumin, coriander, chili powder, salt, crushed lime leaves, and mustard seeds.
Heat the oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Once hot, add the mustard seeds and let them pop.
Add the fresh lime juice to the hot oil and stir for 30 s to release aromas.
Pour the tadka (spiced oil) over the dry mixture, tossing gently to coat all the crumbs.
Transfer the crumble mixture to a parchment‑lined baking dish.
Bake for 8‑10 minutes, stirring every 3 minutes, until the crumble turns a golden brown and the coconut is toasted.
Remove from oven and let cool slightly. The crumble will firm up as it cools.
Serve the crispy tangy crumble as a topping over low‑fat Greek yogurt or a light salad, or enjoy it as a crunchy snack.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
